title: fix: faster Sirius elevator models + in-character LLM errors
author: voodoohop
pr_body_excerpt:
The elevator game felt "awfully slow". Root cause: DeepSeek (the default) is a reasoning model, and `reasoning_effort: "low"` still let it think for thousands of tokens — on the long Guide prompt (the game's first request) replies went **past 10s** in benchmarks against `gen.pollinations.ai`.

Benchmarked the free models on the game's **real** prompts (Guide + elevator floor-3 mid-game), 3 runs each, 10s cap:

- `deepseek` + `low` (shipped): **timed out 3/3** on the Guide prompt
- `mistral` / `openai` / `gemini-fast`: ~1.0–1.4s, 3/3 valid JSON

Changes:
- Default model → `mistral` (fast, reliable JSON); dropdown trimmed to mistral / openai / deepseek / claude-fast
- `reasoning_effort: "none"` for `deepseek` (kept as an option for its in-character resistance); `"low"` for the others (no-op for non-reasoning models)
- LLM errors now surface the **real upstream error** inside an in-character elevator line instead of a generic apology

Verified: tsc + vite build pass (170kB), biome clean, mistral returns valid JSON live, error extraction pulls the clean `error.message` from a real gateway 400.

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

gist.headline: Siriusly fast elevators
gist.blurb: The Sirius Cybernetics Elevator Challenge just got a major speed boost. We swapped the default model to Mistral for lightning-fast replies, and even the error messages are now fully in-character.
gist.summary: Updated the Sirius Cybernetics Elevator Challenge app to use Mistral as the default model instead of DeepSeek, reducing response times from over 10 seconds to ~1.2 seconds, and wrapped upstream API errors in themed in-character dialogue.
gist.impact: Players will experience near-instant elevator responses and see actual API error details delivered with the elevator's signature personality.
gist.visual_concept: Polly the bee riding a retro-futuristic elevator, looking at a fast-moving floor indicator. The Monitor Robot is riding along, displaying a lightning bolt for speed.
gist.image_prompt: Cozy pixel art scene of the cute bee mascot wearing a tiny bellhop hat, riding inside a retro-futuristic elevator with glowing lime green (#ecf874) buttons and a fast-moving floor indicator. A chunky robot with a CRT monitor head stands next to her, screen showing a lightning bolt. Soft pastel gradient background through the glass elevator doors. Warm lighting, lo-fi 8-bit aesthetic, Stardew Valley vibes. Nostalgic but beautiful, emotionally warm.
